service.name: MusicBrainz
service.description: Open music encyclopedia providing structured metadata about artists, releases, recordings, labels, works, and more. Supports three core operations per entity: lookup by MBID, Lucene-powered search, and browse of linked entities with pagination. Also supports non-MBID lookups by disc ID, ISRC, and ISWC. All responses are JSON when fmt=json is specified. Requires a descriptive User-Agent header; requests without one are rejected with HTTP 403. Rate-limited to 1 request per second per IP.

schemes.description: No authentication for read-only GET requests. A custom User-Agent header is MANDATORY (e.g., 'MyApp/1.0 (contact@example.com)'). Requests with a generic or missing User-Agent receive HTTP 403. POST requests (tag/rating/ISRC/barcode submission) require OAuth or HTTP digest authentication.

tools.name: lookup_area
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific area by MBID. Areas represent geographic regions (countries, cities, subdivisions). Supports inc= for aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and relationship types (*-rels).
tools.when_to_use: When you have an area MBID and want details about that geographic region, its ISO codes, or its relationships to other entities.

inc.description: Subqueries joined with '+'. Valid: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and any *-rels (e.g., area-rels, artist-rels, url-rels).

tools.name: lookup_artist
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific artist by MBID. Supports subquery inc= to include linked entities: recordings, releases, release-groups, works. Also supports aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and all *-rels. When including releases or release-groups, filter with type= and status=. Linked entities capped at 25; use browse for full lists.
tools.when_to_use: When you have an artist MBID and want full details, optionally including their releases, works, tags, or relationships.

inc.description: Subqueries joined with '+'. Entity includes: recordings, releases, release-groups, works. Modifiers: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, discids, media, isrcs, artist-credits, various-artists. Rels: area-rels, artist-rels, event-rels, label-rels, place-rels, recording-rels, release-rels, release-group-rels, series-rels, url-rels, work-rels.

type.description: Filter included release-groups/releases by type: album, single, ep, broadcast, other, compilation, live, remix, soundtrack, etc.

status.description: Filter included releases by status: official, promotion, bootleg, pseudo-release, withdrawn, cancelled.

type.description: Person, Group, Orchestra, Choir, Character, Other.

errors.description: Invalid MBID or inc combination. Cannot combine recordings and releases in one lookup.
Artist not found. Search by name first.

tools.name: lookup_event
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific event by MBID. Events represent concerts, festivals, etc. Supports inc= for aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have an event MBID and want details about a concert, festival, or other music event.

inc.description: Valid: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.

tools.name: lookup_genre
tools.description: Retrieves information about a specific genre by MBID. No inc parameters supported.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a genre MBID and want its details.

tools.name: lookup_instrument
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific instrument by MBID. Supports inc= for aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have an instrument MBID and want its description and relationships.

tools.name: lookup_label
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific label by MBID. Supports inc= for releases (max 25, filterable by type/status), aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a label MBID and want details about the record label, optionally including its releases.

inc.description: Entity: releases. Modifiers: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings. Rels: *-rels.

tools.name: lookup_place
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific place by MBID. Places represent venues, studios, etc. Supports inc= for aliases, annotation, tags, genres, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a place MBID and want details about a venue, studio, or location.

tools.name: lookup_recording
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a recording by MBID. A recording is a unique audio entity. Supports inc= for: releases, release-groups, artists, artist-credits, isrcs, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and all *-rels including work-rels and work-level-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a recording MBID and want details — artist credits, ISRCs, tags, releases it appears on, or related works.

inc.description: Entity: releases, release-groups. Modifiers: artists, artist-credits, isrcs, annotation, tags, genres, ratings. Rels: *-rels, work-level-rels.

tools.name: lookup_release
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a release by MBID. A release is a specific edition of a release group. Supports inc= for: collections, labels, recordings (full tracklist), release-groups, artist-credits, discids, media, isrcs (requires recordings), annotation, tags, genres, and all *-rels including recording-level-rels, release-group-level-rels, work-level-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a release MBID and want its full details — tracklist, labels, artist credits, disc IDs, or relationships.

inc.description: Entity: collections, labels, recordings, release-groups. Modifiers: artist-credits, discids, media, isrcs, annotation, tags, genres. Rels: *-rels, recording-level-rels, release-group-level-rels, work-level-rels.

media.description: Array of mediums, each with tracks (when inc=recordings). Tracks contain recording objects with MBIDs.

tools.name: lookup_release_group
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a release group by MBID. A release group is an abstract 'album concept'. Supports inc= for: releases (max 25, filterable), artist-credits, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a release group MBID and want its details — types, related releases, or genres.

inc.description: Entity: releases. Modifiers: artist-credits, annotation, tags, genres, ratings. Rels: *-rels.

tools.name: lookup_series
tools.description: Retrieves information about a series by MBID. Supports inc= for aliases, annotation, tags, genres, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a series MBID and want details about an ordered grouping of related entities.

tools.name: lookup_work
tools.description: Retrieves information about a work by MBID. A work represents a distinct composition. Supports inc= for aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a work MBID and want details about a composition — writers, ISWCs, or relationships.

tools.name: lookup_url
tools.description: Retrieves information about a URL entity by MBID. Supports *-rels to see what entities link to this URL.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a URL entity MBID and want to see its relationships.

tools.name: lookup_url_by_resource
tools.description: Looks up URL entities by actual URL string (not MBID). Accepts one or more 'resource' parameters (up to 100). URLs not found are skipped. The resource value must be URL-encoded.
tools.when_to_use: When you have an actual URL (e.g., artist website, Spotify link) and want to find the corresponding MusicBrainz URL entity.

tools.name: list_all_genres
tools.description: Returns all MusicBrainz genres in alphabetical order, paginated.
tools.when_to_use: When you want the complete list of valid MusicBrainz genres.

tools.name: lookup_discid
tools.description: Looks up releases by Disc ID. Supports 'toc' for fuzzy matching. Use '-' as discid for TOC-only lookup. Pass cdstubs=no to skip CD stubs. Inc params match release lookup. Set media-format=all to include non-CD media in TOC fuzzy lookup.
tools.when_to_use: When you have a CD disc ID or TOC and want to find matching releases.

discid.description: Disc ID string or '-' for TOC-only.

tools.name: lookup_isrc
tools.description: Looks up recordings by ISRC code. Returns all recordings linked to the ISRC. Inc params match recording lookup.
tools.when_to_use: When you have an ISRC and want to find which recordings it identifies.

tools.name: lookup_iswc
tools.description: Looks up works by ISWC code. Returns all works linked to the ISWC. Inc params match work lookup.
tools.when_to_use: When you have an ISWC and want to find which works it identifies.

tools.name: search_annotations
tools.description: Searches the annotation index. Fields: entity (MBID), id, name, text, type. Default: name, text, type.
tools.when_to_use: When looking for free-text annotations attached to MusicBrainz entities.

tools.name: search_areas
tools.description: Searches the area index. Fields: aid, alias, area, areaaccent, begin, comment, end, ended, iso, iso1, iso2, iso3, sortname, tag, type. Default: area.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a geographic area by name, ISO code, or type.

tools.name: search_artists
tools.description: Searches the artist index. Fields: alias, primary_alias, area, arid, artist, artistaccent, begin, beginarea, comment, country, end, endarea, ended, gender, ipi, isni, sortname, tag, type. Default: alias, artist, sortname.
tools.when_to_use: When finding an artist by name, country, type, tag, or other criteria. Use to resolve names to MBIDs.

query.description: Lucene query. Ex: 'radiohead', 'artist:bjork AND type:person AND country:IS'.

tools.name: search_cdstubs
tools.description: Searches the CD stub index. Fields: added, artist, barcode, comment, discid, title, tracks. Default: artist, title.
tools.when_to_use: When looking for CD stubs by artist, title, barcode, or disc ID.

tools.name: search_events
tools.description: Searches the event index. Fields: alias, aid, area, arid, artist, begin, comment, end, ended, eid, event, eventaccent, pid, place, tag, type. Default: alias, artist, event.
tools.when_to_use: When finding concerts, festivals, or events by name, artist, place, or date.

tools.name: search_instruments
tools.description: Searches the instrument index. Fields: alias, comment, description, iid, instrument, instrumentaccent, tag, type. Default: alias, description, instrument.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a musical instrument by name, type, or description.

tools.name: search_labels
tools.description: Searches the label index. Fields: alias, area, begin, code, comment, country, end, ended, ipi, isni, label, labelaccent, laid, release_count, sortname, tag, type. Default: alias, label.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a record label by name, country, label code, or type.

tools.name: search_places
tools.description: Searches the place index. Fields: address, alias, area, begin, comment, end, ended, lat, long, place, placeaccent, pid, type. Default: address, alias, area, place.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a venue, studio, or place by name, address, or area.

tools.name: search_recordings
tools.description: Searches the recording index. Fields: alias, arid, artist, artistname, comment, country, creditname, date, dur, firstreleasedate, format, isrc, number, position, primarytype, qdur, recording, recordingaccent, reid, release, rgid, rid, secondarytype, status, tag, tid, tnum, tracks, tracksrelease, type, video. Default: recording.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a recording by name, artist, ISRC, duration, or other criteria.

tools.name: search_releases
tools.description: Searches the release index. Fields: arid, artist, artistname, asin, barcode, catno, comment, country, creditname, date, discids, discidsmedium, format, laid, label, lang, mediums, packaging, primarytype, quality, reid, release, releaseaccent, rgid, script, secondarytype, status, tag, tracks, tracksmedium, type. Default: release.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a release by title, barcode, catalog number, label, or artist.

tools.name: search_release_groups
tools.description: Searches the release-group index. Fields: alias, arid, artist, artistname, comment, creditname, firstreleasedate, primarytype, reid, release, releasegroup, releasegroupaccent, releases, rgid, secondarytype, status, tag, type. Default: releasegroup.
tools.when_to_use: When finding an album/single/EP concept by title, artist, or type.

tools.name: search_series
tools.description: Searches the series index. Fields: alias, comment, orderingtype, series, seriesaccent, sid, tag, type. Default: alias, series.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a series of related works, events, releases, or recordings.

tools.name: search_tags
tools.description: Searches the tag index. Fields: tag. Default: tag.
tools.when_to_use: When finding tags used in MusicBrainz by keyword.

tools.name: search_urls
tools.description: Searches the URL index. Fields: relationtype, targetid, targettype, uid, url. Default: url.
tools.when_to_use: When finding URL entities by URL string or relationship type.

tools.name: search_works
tools.description: Searches the work index. Fields: alias, arid, artist, comment, iswc, lang, tag, type, wid, work, workaccent. Default: alias, artist, work.
tools.when_to_use: When finding a composition by title, composer, ISWC, or language.

tools.name: browse_areas
tools.description: Browses areas linked to a collection. Pagination via limit/offset. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing all areas in a collection.

tools.name: browse_artists
tools.description: Browses artists linked to another entity. Linked: area, collection, recording, release, release-group, work. Provide exactly ONE. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing all artists linked to a specific area, recording, release, release-group, work, or collection.

errors.description: Exactly one linked entity param required.

tools.name: browse_collections
tools.description: Browses collections linked to another entity. Linked: area, artist, editor (username), event, label, place, recording, release, release-group, work. Provide exactly ONE. Add inc=user-collections (auth required) for private collections.
tools.when_to_use: When listing collections containing a specific entity, or a user's collections.

tools.name: browse_events
tools.description: Browses events linked to another entity. Linked: area, artist, collection, place. Provide exactly ONE. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing events in an area, by an artist, at a place, or in a collection.

tools.name: browse_genres
tools.description: Browses genres in a collection.
tools.when_to_use: When listing all genres in a collection.

tools.name: browse_instruments
tools.description: Browses instruments in a collection. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres.
tools.when_to_use: When listing all instruments in a collection.

tools.name: browse_labels
tools.description: Browses labels linked to another entity. Linked: area, collection, release. Provide exactly ONE. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing labels from a specific area, in a collection, or on a release.

tools.name: browse_places
tools.description: Browses places linked to an area or collection. Provide exactly ONE. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing venues/studios in an area or collection.

tools.name: browse_recordings
tools.description: Browses recordings linked to another entity. Linked: artist, collection, release, work. Provide exactly ONE. Inc: artist-credits, isrcs, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When you want the full paginated list of recordings by an artist, on a release, of a work, or in a collection.

inc.description: Valid: artist-credits, isrcs, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.

tools.name: browse_releases
tools.description: Browses releases linked to another entity. Linked: area, artist, collection, label, track, track_artist, recording, release-group. Provide exactly ONE. Supports type= and status= filters. Inc: artist-credits, labels, recordings, release-groups, media, discids, isrcs (with recordings), annotation, tags, genres, and *-rels. track_artist returns releases where artist appears on track credits only (various-artist appearances). Paging caveat: server limits to 500 total tracks per page.
tools.when_to_use: When you want the full paginated releases by an artist, on a label, in a release-group, etc. Use when lookup inc=releases (max 25) is insufficient.

status.description: Filter: official, promotion, bootleg, pseudo-release, withdrawn, cancelled.

tools.name: browse_release_groups
tools.description: Browses release groups linked to another entity. Linked: artist, collection, release. Provide exactly ONE. Supports type= filter. Inc: artist-credits, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels. When browsing by artist, supports release-group-status filter (website-default|all).
tools.when_to_use: When you want the full paginated discography (release groups) for an artist, or all release groups in a collection.

tools.name: browse_series
tools.description: Browses series in a collection. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing all series in a collection.

tools.name: browse_works
tools.description: Browses works linked to an artist or collection. Provide exactly ONE. Inc: aliases, annotation, tags, genres, ratings, and *-rels.
tools.when_to_use: When listing all works (compositions) by an artist or in a collection.

examples.description: Full artist discography workflow: search → release groups → releases → tracklist → recording detail

steps.note: Search for the artist to resolve name to MBID.
Browse full album discography as release groups.
Browse all official editions of the first album.
Get full tracklist with recording MBIDs.
Look up a track's recording for ISRCs, tags, and linked works.

examples.description: Identify a recording by ISRC and find which releases contain it
Find all live bootleg releases by an artist
Find a label and browse its catalog
Resolve a Spotify URL to a MusicBrainz entity

steps.note: Find which artist links to this Spotify URL.

knowledge.system_context:
MusicBrainz is an open music encyclopedia with a strict entity hierarchy:

Artist -> Release Group -> Release -> Medium -> Track -> Recording

Key entities: Artist (person/group), Release Group (abstract album concept), Release (specific edition), Medium (disc/side), Track (position on medium), Recording (unique audio, carries ISRCs), Work (composition, carries ISWCs), Label, Event, Place, Area (geography, ISO codes), Series, Genre, URL.

Three core API operations:
- Lookup (GET /entity/{mbid}): One entity by MBID. inc= for related data, CAPPED AT 25 linked entities.
- Browse (GET /entity?linked_entity={mbid}): ALL linked entities, fully paginated (limit+offset). Use when 25-cap is insufficient.
- Search (GET /entity?query=...): Lucene full-text search with ranked results.

Critical quirks:
1. inc= on lookup caps at 25 linked entities. Always use browse for complete lists.
2. Browse release paging: 500-track ceiling per page. Increment offset by actual count, not limit.
3. fmt=json must be passed as query param or Accept header. XML is default.
4. User-Agent header MANDATORY. Generic agents get HTTP 403.
5. Rate limit: 1 req/sec. Exceeding -> HTTP 503 or IP ban.
6. Relationships: inc= like 'artist-rels', 'url-rels'. For sub-entity rels: 'recording-level-rels', 'work-level-rels'.
7. type= and status= filters only affect responses that include releases or release-groups.
8. Search uses Lucene syntax: field:value, AND/OR/NOT, wildcards (*/?), fuzzy (~), phrases.
9. Same recording can appear across many releases — it's the audio, not the physical track.
10. Works = compositions (songs), distinct from recordings (performances). Link via work-rels.

workflows.name: artist_discography
workflows.trigger: When a user asks for an artist's albums, discography, or complete releases.
steps: search_artists to resolve name to MBID
browse_release_groups with artist= and type=album for full discography
browse_releases with release-group= for all editions of an album
workflows.interpretation: Release groups are album concepts; releases are specific editions. Always browse release-groups first.

workflows.name: track_identification
workflows.trigger: When a user has a song name or ISRC and wants to identify it.
workflows.interpretation: Multiple recordings may share a title. Use disambiguation, artist-credit, and first-release-date to identify.

glossary.MBID: MusicBrainz Identifier — UUID uniquely identifying any entity.
glossary.Release: A specific edition — pressing, remaster, or digital release.
glossary.Recording: Unique audio entity. Same recording across many tracks/releases.
glossary.Track: Numbered entry on a medium, links to a recording.
glossary.Medium: Physical disc or digital container within a release.
glossary.Work: Distinct composition, independent of any recording. Carries ISWCs.
glossary.ISRC: International Standard Recording Code — 12-char code for sound recordings.
glossary.ISWC: International Standard Musical Work Code — identifies compositions.
glossary.Disc ID: Hash from CD table of contents.
glossary.inc parameter: Include param for additional linked data. Multiple values joined with '+'.
glossary.CD Stub: Minimal release entry from disc ID submission, not yet linked to full data.
glossary.Secondary Type: Additional classification: Compilation, Live, Soundtrack, Remix, etc.
